body.title: Suppress raw 'Background job complete' user-visible echo when a reply-obligation bouquet was pushed
body.result: b638c6db — Suppression check before channel post block. When item.goal_id linked to awaiting_reply goal, sets _suppress_user_echo=True and skips cc/web/discord channel posts. Bouquet push and ring write still run. Errors bypass suppression. Legacy forks without goal_id keep existing echo. New stage echo_suppressed in reply_obligations.log. 6 new tests + 23 related tests pass. Closes the half-fix from 59a7184c.
body.description:
T-reply-obligation-fork (59a7184c) added the bouquet-to-TWM pattern at job_completions drain so the next turn's reasoning has the result + origin question + pending_reply marker. This works at the cognition layer.

BUT: I never suppressed the existing user-visible 'Background job complete: **<title>**' channel post on main.py:6385-6399. So the user sees the raw fork echo PLUS gets a (better) reply on the next turn. The bouquet helps the next turn but doesn't replace the immediate noise. Akien observed this in 2026-04-13 transcript:

  Igor: Thinking about that...
  Igor: Background job complete: **[Web message from akien]: why would we write your basic six **

The second message is the raw echo we should have suppressed. The bouquet that fired on the same completion is what enables the next-turn reply, but the user never wanted to see the echo.

FIX: when item.get('goal_id') is present AND the linked goal has awaiting_reply=true, SUPPRESS the channel post + web_server.send for the raw fork echo. The bouquet handles next-turn reasoning; the user's experience should be 'Igor said thinking about that → next reply addresses the question' with no debug noise in between.

Preserve the existing behavior for fork completions WITHOUT a reply-obligation goal — those still emit the raw echo because there's no bouquet to take its place.

INTERACTION WITH T-reply-obligation-fork: this is the missing half. T-reply-obligation-fork added the bouquet (next-turn cognition); this removes the user-visible noise (current-turn UX). Both halves are needed for the full fix.

FORENSIC: extend reply_obligations.log with a new stage 'echo_suppressed' so we can see how often the suppression fires.

INERTIA: LOW. Single conditional in main.py:6385 area.
